pr.title: Split out the majority of the mn related files
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly organization PR that split most menu (`mn`) code out of the monolithic `asm/melee/text_3.s` into dedicated `asm/melee/mn/*.s` files, added those object files to `obj_files.mk`, moved a stage-related file to `asm/melee/gr/stage.s`, and adjusted section address comments. This did not appear to decompile code into C; it primarily made existing assembly easier to navigate and prepared the menu subsystem for future matching/decomp work.
what_changed: Removed most of a very large assembly block from `asm/melee/text_3.s` and redistributed it into subsystem-specific assembly files under `asm/melee/mn/`.

Created many menu assembly units named after inferred menu modules: `mncharsel`, `mncount`, `mndatadel`, `mndeflicker`, `mnevent`, `mngallery`, `mnhyaku`, `mninfo`, `mninfobonus`, `mnitemsw`, `mnlanguage`, `mnname`, `mnsnap`, `mnsound`, `mnsoundtest`, `mnstagesel`, `mnstagesw`, and `mnvibration`.

Added `asm/melee/mn/code_8023B0F8.s`, a still-address-named menu assembly file covering `.text` range `0x8023B0F8 - 0x802474C0`; the diff excerpt shows functions such as `func_8023B0F8`, `func_8023B224`, and `func_8023B314` remained as raw assembly labels.
Added a large `asm/melee/text_4.s` file, indicating some residual monolithic text content remained split by address/text segment rather than fully named subsystem modules.
Updated `obj_files.mk` with 23 added lines and 1 deletion so the newly split assembly files were built as separate objects.

Renamed/moved `asm/melee/code_80224A54.s` to `asm/melee/gr/stage.s` with 99% similarity and changed the section comment from the broad text range `0x80005940 - 0x803B7240` to the narrower `0x80224A54 - 0x80225370`.
No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided context.
smart_moves: Used subsystem directory structure (`asm/melee/mn/` and `asm/melee/gr/`) to turn monolithic assembly dumps into files that reflect game systems, improving searchability and future ownership of decomp tasks.

Kept exact assembly content intact while moving it, minimizing behavioral risk; the changed-file stats are dominated by deletions from `text_3.s` and additions to new `.s` files rather than semantic edits.
Preserved address-based fallback naming where a better module name was apparently not yet known (`code_8023B0F8.s`), avoiding overclaiming names while still isolating a coherent address range.
Updated `.section .text` comments with precise address spans for split files, which helps maintain map/order awareness during later matching work.
Updated `obj_files.mk` in the same PR as the file split, keeping the build graph aligned with the new assembly layout.
decomp_lessons: Before converting a large subsystem to C, splitting assembly by inferred module boundaries can reduce navigation cost and make future matching PRs smaller and easier to review.
When confidence in a source file's real name is limited, an address-based filename such as `code_8023B0F8.s` is preferable to inventing an unsupported module name.
Assembly-only split PRs should preserve labels, instructions, and object order while changing only paths/build lists; this keeps matching stable while improving repo organization.
Section comments with exact address ranges are useful metadata after splitting monolithic assembly because they document the linker/order boundary each file represents.

Menu subsystem files in this project follow the short `mn` prefix convention, so future menu decomp files should likely retain names such as `mncharsel`, `mnstagesel`, `mnitemsw`, and `mnsoundtest` unless stronger evidence supersedes them.
naming_conventions: Menu subsystem files use the `mn` prefix and compact lowercase module names: `mncharsel`, `mnstagesel`, `mnstagesw`, `mnitemsw`, `mnsoundtest`, etc.
Stage/ground-related assembly was placed under `asm/melee/gr/`, with `code_80224A54.s` renamed to the descriptive path `gr/stage.s`.
Unknown or insufficiently identified modules can retain address-based names using `code_<start address>.s`, as with `asm/melee/mn/code_8023B0F8.s`.
Raw unmatched functions and labels retained address-derived names such as `func_8023B0F8`, `func_8023B224`, `lbl_8023B138`, and `lbl_804DBF20`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Performed a file split/rename without apparent instruction-level changes, a low-risk tactic for organizing unmatched assembly while preserving matching behavior.
Maintained `.include "macros.inc"` and `.section .text` declarations at the top of newly split assembly files.
Kept global function labels and local labels unchanged after moving code, preserving branch/call symbol references.
Used precise `.text` section range comments to document extracted address ranges, e.g. `0x8023B0F8 - 0x802474C0` for `mn/code_8023B0F8.s` and `0x80224A54 - 0x80225370` for `gr/stage.s`.
Synchronized new assembly file boundaries with `obj_files.mk`, which is essential for keeping linker order and object inclusion correct after a split.
searchable_terms: Split out the majority of the mn related files
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that decompile files under `src/melee/mn/` or replace `asm/melee/mn/*.s` files introduced by PR 77.
Search for when `asm/melee/mn/code_8023B0F8.s` receives a descriptive name or is split further.
Check later changes to `obj_files.mk` for the transition from these assembly object files to C object files.
Search for PRs touching `asm/melee/gr/stage.s` or corresponding `gr/stage` C files to see how the stage split was used.
